text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
...
That should do it.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
One day, Air Shakur shows up at my office with a
large cardboard box.
choice_data_list: Um... Shakur? What is this exactly?

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
It's yours.
Keep what ya want and chuck whatever you don't.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I don't really understand, but I accept it for now
and then check the contents. Everything is still
sealed. It seems she hasn't touched any of it since
it was delivered.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The contents are absolute chaos. At first, I think
it's just crammed full of junk food, but then I
notice some healthy, organic food mixed in there
too.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
There are "health drinks" with no scientific
backing mixed in with the latest scientific magazines.
I also find what appears to be a child's towel as well
as a stylish handkerchief. Amid this assortment of
seemingly random items, something catches my eye.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
It shows a youthful yet frail Umamusume cradling
a child in what looks to be a bar. There's a
message on the back of the photo.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
That's right.
It's all from my mom.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
My curiosity gets the best of me a few days later,
and Air Shakur lightly acknowledges it was a gift
from home.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
It's all about what I expected. Man, she's so
pathetic.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
She just wants to give the appearance of being a
parent. That's why there's no rhyme or reason to
what she sent.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
I mean, a kids towel and a baby picture? I
imagine that's her way of saying she wants things
to go back to how they used to be.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
Before I was old enough to know any better, my
mom dressed me up in whatever she liked. She
just misses the days when I went along with
everything like a good little girl.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
This is not her typical tone. I can gather from her
open aggression that there's a deep rift between
her and her mother.
choice_data_list:
(If I tell her about the message, it might just
add fuel to the fire...)

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The message on the back of the photograph of
the two reads: "At the very least, tell me what you
want me to do. Try to understand my feelings."

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
I can't even remember the last time we spoke
properly. You'd think she'd take the hint by now
that I'm through with her.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: Besides... she was the first to throw in the towel. She should have just ended it there.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
A possible reason for all the random items strikes
me. She may have no idea what Air Shakur
actually needs. This could be the result of her
agonizing over every possibility.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
"Try to understand my feelings." I imagine this
might be a plea for not only understanding, but
for a chance to speak.
choice_data_list: Yeah, it seems like she doesn't get you. But...

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: But what?
choice_data_list:
It could be a sign that she wants to do
something for you.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
Yeah, right.
And even if that was the case, I don't need her
forcing anything on me.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Not wanting to intrude any further, I decide to
change the subject.
choice_data_list: At any rate, she sure sent a bunch of stuff...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Not wanting to intrude any further, I decide to
change the subject.
choice_data_list: At any rate, she sure sent a bunch of stuff...

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: Just some bar she used to work at.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
As if she could do that without making a mess of
everything.
No, she was a singer.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
She sat in the corner and sang for everyone while
they drank.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
So her mother was a singer. I'm interested in
hearing more, but Air Shakur doesn't seem to
want to continue the discussion.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I take another look at the photo. Her mother has
a gentle look about her, while Air Shakur looks
like she was as uncompromising as a kid as she is
now.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: All kids look more or less the same at that age.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: Ah... Just look at that outfit. Freakin' frill city.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
I hated it so much that I tore it to shreds one day
when she wasn't around. I remember her crying
her eyes out after.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
And the way she cried... looking right at me with
tears streaming down her face... I couldn't stand
it.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I wonder if Air Shakur and her mother will have a
chance to talk sometime in the future.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
It seems like too much to have come from just a
package or two. I suspect that...
choice_data_list:
Have you never opened a single thing she's sent
you?

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: Of course not.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: No, hang on. I opened one.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: Well, Doto did. Not me.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
Yeah. Y'know, same room? Apparently, she
thought it was for her.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
No idea why, but apparently my mom sent some
super spicy snacks. Anyway, Doto wolfed them all
down.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
She practically begged for me to forgive her when
she realized. She wouldn't stop moping. Can you
imagine how annoying it is to deal with that in
your own room?

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
I told her she did me a favor since I didn't even
want them in the first place, and I'd give her even
more. That made her happy. Too happy...

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: She started talking to me all the time after that.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: Weirdo.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I imagine scenes like that are not altogether
uncommon in their dorm room, and I get the
feeling I've caught a glimpse of the reason why
Meisho Doto is so attached to Air Shakur.
